page: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight exceptional single-core and specialized compute capabilities. The c8a.4xlarge achieves top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, and single-threaded compression tasks such as gzip and bzip3. It also demonstrates top-tier efficiency in image and text processing, alongside high-speed prompt processing and text generation for small-scale language models. Multi-core stress-ng performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and medium-scale LLM inference speeds remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.4xlarge offers high compute density and modern DDR5 memory speeds, making it highly resource-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This instance is highly suited for high-performance web serving, single-threaded data processing, batch computing, and lightweight machine learning inference.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in single-core operations, Geekbench scores, image processing, and small-scale LLM inference, while multi-core and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks sit in the average tier. This hardware profile delivers high compute density, making it cost-efficient for CPU-intensive tasks. It is well-suited for web serving, batch processing, and lightweight inference workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for intensive single-core and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and small LLM inference performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Optimized for web serving, batch processing, and CPU-bound workloads
